capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole chip from a P300c endpoint (TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0)
capacity_evidence.stage_scope: The correctness-first translation preloads a dense causal mask. At the advertised length that mask alone exceeds allocator-reported DRAM, before the 1.6 GB BF16 decoder weights, input, KV cache, outputs, and temporaries.
capacity_evidence.unprobed_interval: 18 through 131071; current_supported_context is a validation boundary, not a claimed physical maximum

notes: This functional starting point validates prefill through the emitted sequence length 17 and decode at position 17. Its allocated cache/mask extent is 128, but prefill S=128 was not validated and 128 is not claimed as a physical limit. The dense-mask design is proven infeasible at the HF target; intermediate lengths were not capacity-probed. A different long-context memory policy is deferred.

emit_provenance.collective_collapse: Full Hugging Face weights replace emitted shards; attention and expert all-reduces plus routing mesh partition are absent from runtime.

dtype_policy.functional_baseline: BF16 weights, activations, and cache; TILE layout; DRAM by default
dtype_policy.exception: The emitted router promotes its activation and linear result to FP32 before BF16 top-k; decode heads use one workload-derived L1 height shard.
dtype_policy.deferred: Restoring emitted BF8 projection/cache precision belongs to a later datatype-selection stage.
